events.title: CrossFit Games
events.address: 525 W Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95113
events.description: Parking for the 2026 CrossFit Games Parking Attending Multiple Days? The 3-Day Parking Pass is the recommended option for guests attending more than one day of the CrossFit Games. Enjoy the convenience of a single parking purchase for the entire weekend, guaranteed access to the ABC Lot, and the flexibility to come and go as needed throughout the event. 3-Day Parking Pass (ABC Lot) Guaranteed parking access in the ABC Lot all weekend Unlimited in-and-out privileges throughout the event One parking credential valid for all three event days No need to purchase parking separately each day Exclusive access to the ABC Lot Pricing Advance Purchase: $150 + applicable fees Event-Day Rate (if available): $180 Please note: ABC Lot inventory is limited and may sell out before the event. Advance purchase is strongly recommended. Purchase All 3 Days Vip Parking Package (includes In and Out Privileges in the A-B-C Main Lot) Attending a Single Day? Single-day parking is available in the Delmas Lots. Single-Day Parking Pass (Delmas Lots) Valid only on the selected event day Parking access from 8 AM to 7 PM No in-and-out privileges Re-entry requires a new parking purchase Pricing Advance Purchase: $45 + applicable fees Event-Day Rate: $50 Purchase General Event Parking for Friday, 7/24 Purchase General Event Parking for Saturday, 7/25 Purchase General Event Parking for Sunday 7/26 Why Purchase Parking in Advance? Parking inventory for the CrossFit Games weekend is limited. Purchasing parking before arriving is the easiest way to ensure a smooth and stress-free event experience. Benefits of purchasing in advance: Secure your parking before lots reach capacity Avoid searching for available parking on event day Speed up your arrival experience Guarantee access to your selected parking option Guests attending multiple days are encouraged to purchase the 3-Day Parking Pass early, as ABC Lot inventory is limited.
events.blurb: Watch elite athletes compete in the CrossFit Games.

Advance purchase is strongly recommended. Purchase All 3 Days Vip Parking Package (includes In and Out Privileges in the A-B-C Main Lot) Attending a Single Day? Single-day parking is available in the Delmas Lots. Single-Day Parking Pass (Delmas Lots) Valid only on the selected event day Parking access from 8 AM to 7 PM No in-and-out privileges Re-entry requires a new parking purchase Pricing Advance Purchase: $45 + applicable fees Event-Day Rate: $50 Purchase General Event Parking for Friday, 7/24 Purchase General Event Parking for Saturday, 7/25 Purchase General Event Parking for Sunday 7/26 Why Purchase Parking in Advance? Parking inventory for the CrossFit Games weekend is limited. Purchasing parking before arriving is the easiest way to ensure a smooth and stress-free event experience. Benefits of purchasing in advance: Secure your parking before lots reach capacity Avoid searching for available parking on event day Speed up your arrival experience Guarantee access to your selected parking option Guests attending multiple days are encouraged to purchase the 3-Day Parking Pass early, as ABC Lot inventory is limited.
events.blurb: Watch elite athletes compete in the CrossFit Games.

events.title: Middle School Bike Skills
events.address: Cupertino Senior Center, 21251 Stevens Creek Blvd, 95014
events.description: Middle School Bike Skills workshops prepare rising 6th, 7th, and 8th graders to bike to middle school and beyond. The original course teaches students and their parent or guardian safe biking fundamentals. New this year is a sequel which teaches students how to adapt those skills to stay safe while riding an e-bike and a third course teaching students the basics of maintaining and doing simple repairs on their own bikes. Msbs 101 - Middle School Bike Workshop Msbs 102 - e-Bike Workshop Msbs 103 - Bike Maintenance and Repair Workshop This Middle School Bike Skills program is brought to you by Cupertino Safe Routes to School in partnership with Wheel Kids. Registration is required. Links and more information can be found at www.cupertino.gov/saferoutes/msbs
events.blurb: You and your middle schooler learn safe biking fundamentals to ride to school confidently.

events.title: Free Home Composting Education Workshop
events.description: The Uc Cooperative Extension Composting Education Program, sponsored by the Recycling and Waste Reduction Commission of Santa Clara County, offers community classes throughout Santa Clara County that teach residents how to build compost piles and worm bins and apply the compost in their landscape. View additional details about these classes on the Home Compost page. Please note: Composting Basics Workshop scheduled for April 11 is canceled due to rain. Time and location change for the September 26 date.
events.blurb: Learn to build compost piles and worm bins, then use finished compost in your garden.

events.title: The Employee Dharma Handbook
events.description: As an important rocket launch approaches, Val, an HR Executive at an aerospace company in Silicon Valley, suspects that a young engineer was overlooked for a promotion. Leela, a first generation Indian-American, doesn't really want to talk about it, but Val suspects sexism is the cause. What they don't expect to uncover are simmering tensions of ancient origins among the company's Indian immigrant employees…sorry, Valued Team Members. Soon Val is navigating a funhouse of identity, nepotism, and desire as she tries to understand the culture and biases of Leela's managers. When push comes to shove, Val will need to decide what her duty is to Leela, to the company, and to herself.
events.blurb: Watch a play about an HR executive navigating workplace bias at an aerospace company during a pivotal rocket launch.

events.title: Pooch Plunge
events.address: Blackberry Farm Park, 21979 San Fernando Avenue, Cupertino, CA, 95014
events.description: Let your pup take the plunge during a dog-only swim in the Blackberry Farm pools. It's a paws-itively fun way for your pooch to enjoy the pools before they open for summer swimming—tail wags are highly encouraged. Swim Sessions (45 minutes of tail-wagging fun) Block 1 - 9 to 9:45 a.m. Block 2 - 10 to 10:45 a.m. Block 3 - 11 to 11:45 a.m. Block 4 - Noon to 12:45 p.m. Register Registration opens in February for the spring event and in June for the fall event. For additional information, visit the Pooch Plunge webpage.
events.blurb: Your dog swims solo in the pool during a dog-only splash session before summer opens.

events.title: Dracula
events.venue: Mountain View Center for the Performing Arts
events.description: Sink your teeth into the ultimate Halloween thrill. Steven Dietz's adaptation of the timeless classic seduces with elegance and suspense, turning the legendary vampire tale into a high-octane theatrical ride. Bold, chilling, and wickedly smart, the story draws you into a world where reason clashes with superstition and love becomes dangerously intoxicating. This Dracula keeps you leaning forward—never quite sure who holds the power, or what darkness waits just out of sight.
events.blurb: Witness the gothic horror classic rendered as a compelling stage production with talented actors.

Steven Dietz's adaptation of the timeless classic seduces with elegance and suspense, turning the legendary vampire tale into a high-octane theatrical ride. Bold, chilling, and wickedly smart, the story draws you into a world where reason clashes with superstition and love becomes dangerously intoxicating. This Dracula keeps you leaning forward—never quite sure who holds the power, or what darkness waits just out of sight.
events.blurb: Witness the gothic horror classic rendered as a compelling stage production with talented actors.

events.title: Join-A-Ranger: Wildlife Tracking
events.description: Join a Park Ranger for a fun-filled wildlife tracking class. You'll learn about species identification through their tracks and sign-tracking. Become a nature detective and learn to read the ground like a storybook through the art of tracking. Please wear sturdy walking shoes and long pants that can get dirty. All attendees must register and children must be accompanied by an adult.
events.blurb: Learn to identify animal tracks and read the ground like a nature detective with a park ranger.

events.title: Don Giovanni
events.description: DON Giovanni Back after 12 years, Opera San Jose builds a new production of Mozart's dark psychological thriller. Seduction, rebellion, and supernatural justice collide in a thrilling look at a notorious libertine's downfall. The production follows a charismatic anti-hero who pursues pleasure without remorse, leaving a trail of broken hearts and vengeful spirits in a […]
events.blurb: Watch Mozart's dark psychological thriller about a libertine's seduction, rebellion, and supernatural downfall.

events.title: Na Leo Mokupuni in Cupertino
events.description: Na Leo Mokupuni in Cupertino – meaning island sounds in Cupertino Experience the rhythms, songs, and dances of the islands! Na Leo Mokupuni brings together performers to celebrate the vibrant traditions of island music and dance—right here in Cupertino. Preregistration is recommended. Limited tickets available at the door, while supplies last.
events.blurb: Experience island rhythms, songs, and dances rooted in Hawaiian and Pacific island traditions.

events.title: Cupertino Campout
events.address: Creekside Park, 10455 Miller Avenue, Cupertino, CA, 95014
events.description: The Cupertino Campout is a weekend to remember! Families are invited to pitch a tent at Creekside Park and join us for a night under the stars, filled with recreational activities. This campout is a fantastic way to gather with family, neighbors, and friends or make new ones, all while enjoying all kinds of fun. Pre-registration is required.
events.blurb: Pitch a tent and spend the night under the stars with recreational activities and neighbors.

events.title: Breakfast with Santa
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 North Stelling Road, 95014
events.description: You and your family are invited to a festive breakfast celebration with Santa! Enjoy delicious North Pole pancakes prepared by Santa's elves, take photos with Santa, and explore a holiday workshop filled with crafts and activities for children. Three seatings: 8:30 a.m., 9:30 a.m., 10:30 a.m. Register Registration opens in late July.
events.blurb: Enjoy pancakes with Santa, take photos, and do holiday crafts with your family.

events.title: Monster Mash
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 North Stelling Road, Cupertino, CA, 95014
events.description: Get ready for a fang-tastic good time at Cupertino Parks and Recreation's annual Monster Mash Halloween event at the Quinlan Community Center. Put on your favorite costume and enjoy an evening of games, trick-or-treating, spooky crafts, and pumpkin decorating. It's a fun-filled night of Halloween activities for the whole family.
events.blurb: Wear your costume and enjoy games, trick-or-treating, spooky crafts, and pumpkin decorating.

events.title: 2026 Cupertino Community Service Awards
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 N. Stelling Road, 95014
events.description: The third annual Cupertino Community Service Awards will take place on Wednesday, October 28. This ceremony will recognize the 2026 Crest Awards (Cupertino Recognizes Extra Steps Taken), and Commissioners who served on a City Commission. More details and registration information for the October 28 event will be available soon.
events.blurb: Watch the Cupertino Community Service Awards ceremony honoring local volunteers and commissioners.

events.title: Environmental Education Center Open House
events.description: Drop by the Environmental Education Center (EEC) at Mc Clellan Ranch Preserve every Saturday from 11 a.m. to 3 p.m. for family fun and discovery. Meet the City's animal ambassadors, explore local wildlife, engage with preserve staff and park rangers, and enjoy hands-on activities that bring nature to life.
events.blurb: Meet animal ambassadors, learn about local wildlife, and do hands-on activities with park rangers.

events.title: July 4 Symphony in the Park
events.description: Family-friendly Independence Day celebration featuring performances by the San Jose Wind Symphony, a flag raising ceremony, children's bike and stroller parade, carousel, train rides, inflatables, food trucks, and beer and wine garden. Attendees can participate in handwriting the U. S. Constitution.
events.blurb: Watch the San Jose Wind Symphony perform, ride a carousel, and see fireworks light up the park.

events.title: Signing Santa
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 North Stelling Road, 95014
events.description: Deaf and hard of hearing children, along with their families, are invited to spend time with a Santa who understands sign language! Santa only communicates using sign language but an interpreter will be on-site to help translate for friends and family. Register Registration opens in late July.
events.blurb: Meet a signing Santa who communicates entirely in ASL with your deaf or hard-of-hearing children.

events.title: Free Shakespeare in the Park Event
events.description: Free Shakespeare in the Park brings you Anthony and Cleopatra directed by Katja Rivera. Set against the backdrop of the Roman Empire and Egypt, For show details, Anthony and Cleopatra examines the conflict between duty and desire, and the impact of personal relationships. Visit sfshakes.org.
events.blurb: Watch Shakespeare's Anthony and Cleopatra performed outdoors in the amphitheater.

events.title: Handwriting the Constitution
events.venue: Oak Meadow Park and various locations in Los Gatos
events.address: Oak Meadow Park and various locations in Los Gatos
events.description: Community art project celebrating the nation's 250th Sesquicentennial. Participants transcribe portions of the U. S. Constitution on large foam-core whiteboards. Sign up for 90-minute sessions at various locations including the DEI Commission's tables at the July 4 Symphony in the Park.
events.blurb: Transcribe passages from the U.S. Constitution on foam-core boards in a 90-minute community session.

events.title: San José Independence Day Celebration with Drone Show
events.description: Free festival celebrating America's 250th birthday featuring family activities, water slides, food vendors, live entertainment including mariachi and lucha libre, culminating in a professional drone show at 9 p.m. Free shuttle service from Eastridge Center Jc Penney parking lot.
events.blurb: Jump on water slides, catch live mariachi and lucha libre, then watch drones light up the sky at 9 p.m.

events.title: Tree Lighting
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 North Stelling Road, Cupertino, CA, 95014
events.description: Kick off the holiday season at the City's annual Community Tree Lighting! Enjoy the moment the tree is illuminated with hundreds of twinkling lights, live music from a local choir, and festive holiday cheer. After the tree lighting, families can visit Santa and take photos.
events.blurb: Watch the holiday tree light up, hear a local choir sing, and visit Santa for photos.

events.title: Baer Blacksmith Shop Demonstrations
events.description: Community members are invited to visit the Baer Blacksmith Shop from 11 a.m. to 3 p.m. and learn about early Cupertino history, the craft of blacksmithing, and learn from our experienced blacksmith, who will be firing the forge to give demonstrations.
events.blurb: Watch a blacksmith fire the forge and demonstrate the craft while learning early Cupertino history.

events.title: Wildfire preparedness workshop
events.description: Community workshop on wildfire preparedness covering home hardening, defensible space, evacuation planning, and emergency supplies. Includes resource fair with CAL Fire, American Red Cross, PG&E, and local law enforcement representatives.
events.blurb: Get wildfire safety tips and preparedness strategies from Santa Clara County Fire Department staff.

events.title: Pria Anand with Shaili Jain and Tanya Maria Luhrmann
events.description: In the award-winning book The Mind Electric, Pria Anand, a neurologist described by the Times Literary Supplement as "Oliver Sacks' most obvious heir," reckons with the stories we tell about our brains–and the stories our brains tell us.
events.blurb: Hear neurologist Pria Anand discuss how brains shape our stories and ourselves.

events.title: Summer Concert Series
events.address: Memorial Park Amphitheater, 21163 Anton Way, Cupertino, CA, 95014
events.description: Spend your Thursday evenings outdoors with free live music at the Memorial Park Amphitheater. Bring a blanket or low-back chair, settle in on the lawn, and enjoy a lineup of bands spanning classic rock, Latin rock, blues, R&B, and more.
events.blurb: Bring a blanket and catch live music spanning classic rock, Latin, blues, and R&B on the lawn.

events.title: Math Fest
events.venue: Children's Discovery Museum of San Jose
events.address: Children's Discovery Museum of San Jose, 180 Woz Way, San Jose, CA 95110
events.description: A special event in partnership with the Julia Robinson Math Festival featuring interactive hands-on math activities, engaging challenges, and playful exploration designed for children and families. Included with museum admission.
events.blurb: Solve interactive math challenges and hands-on puzzles designed for kids and families.

events.title: Reawakening: Reciprocity of Land, Sea, and the Muwekma Ohlone Tribe
events.address: New Museum Los Gatos, PO Box 1904, Los Gatos, CA 95031
events.description: Exhibition focusing on the Muwekma Ohlone's land stewardship efforts, including cultural burns and native plant cultivation for regalia making, food, and traditional healing, plus cultural revival of language and ceremonies.
events.blurb: Tour an exhibition on Muwekma Ohlone land stewardship, cultural burns, native plant cultivation, and language revival.

events.title: Acga Clay & Glass Festival
events.address: Palo Alto Art Center, 1313 Newell Road, Palo Alto, California 94303
events.description: Annual festival showcasing over 130 California clay and glass artists. Features live artist demonstrations, pottery throwdown competition, hands-on clay activities, food trucks, valet parking, and family fun. Free admission.
events.blurb: Watch live artist demonstrations, see over 130 clay and glass artists, and try hands-on pottery.

events.title: Pal Summer Camps: July 13-17
events.address: Pacific Art League, 668 Ramona Street, Palo Alto, CA 94301
events.description: Art camps for ages 7-16 including Sewing Cozy Critters, Plein Air Drawing & Watercolor, World Builder Studio, and Birdhouse Design & Construction. Professional instructors, small class sizes, high-quality materials included.
events.blurb: Enroll in week-long art camps covering sewing, plein air painting, world building, and construction.

events.title: Brick Blast LEGO Fan Convention
events.address: 5001 Great America Parkway, Santa Clara, CA, 95054
events.description: LEGO fans unite! The Brick Team is bringing a new LEGO fan convention to the Bay Area, featuring creations inspired by space and science fiction to fit the theme "Out of This World." To purchase tickets, click view event.
events.blurb: Browse LEGO creations inspired by space and science fiction at the fan convention.

events.title: Below Market Rate (Bmr) Rental Informational Call
events.description: Informational call about the City's Below-Market Rate Rental Program covering rental opportunities, application process, and how to navigate online portals. Interpretation in Spanish and Mandarin available upon request.
events.blurb: Learn about the city's below-market rental program, application process, and available units.

events.title: 4th of July Community Celebration
events.address: Mission College, 3000 Mission College Blvd, Santa Clara, CA
events.description: Free family-friendly celebration with food trucks, family activities, and live entertainment featuring headliner Pride and Joy. Bring blankets and lawn chairs for a picnic, and enjoy a spectacular fireworks show.
events.blurb: Watch live entertainment and fireworks after eating from food trucks at a free family celebration.

events.title: The Problem with Plastic with Susan Keefe
events.description: Online conversation with Susan Keefe, Beyond Plastics' Southern California Director, discussing the book "The Problem with Plastic" and the environmental and health impacts of plastic pollution and microplastics.
events.blurb: Hear Susan Keefe explain the environmental and health impacts of plastic pollution.

events.title: Planning Commission review of Builder's Remedy projects
events.description: The Planning Commission will review two Builder's Remedy projects: a four-unit single-family development at 14486 Oak Place and a 25-unit development (Masson Estates) on Pierce Road near Saratoga Heights Drive.
events.blurb: Hear the Planning Commission review two Builder's Remedy projects: a four-unit home development and a 25-unit housing site.

events.title: Unlock the Secrets of Seed Saving
events.address: Central Park Library - Edinger Room, 2635 Homestead Road, Santa Clara, California 95051
events.description: Master Gardener talk on harvesting, processing, and storing seeds from your backyard. Learn beginner-friendly techniques for seed preservation, pollination, and growing crops adapted to your local climate.
events.blurb: Learn beginner seed-saving techniques from a Master Gardener expert.

events.title: The Unfinished Business of 1776: Author Talk with Thomas Richards Jr.
events.description: Online conversation with historian Thomas Richards Jr. About his book "The Unfinished Business of 1776," exploring how the American Revolution continues as an ongoing struggle for equality and justice.
events.blurb: Hear historian Thomas Richards Jr. discuss his book on the American Revolution as an ongoing struggle for equality.

events.title: NotebookLM for the Classroom
events.description: Discover how Google's Notebook LM can support your teaching. This workshop introduces the pedagogical potential of Notebook LM as a multimodal AI environment. You will learn how to create Notebooks to…
events.blurb: Learn how to use Google's Notebook LM to enhance your teaching and student learning.

events.address: 345 South First St., San Jose, CA 95113
events.description: Join Densetsu. EXE in their first 3D live! This idol group is ready to take Akiba, and your hearts! See Mint Fantôme, Victoria Roman and Phoebe in this important part of their idol journey. This event…
events.blurb: Watch the idol group Densetsu.EXE perform in their first 3D live show.

events.title: Jane! An intimate exploration of Gilded Age excess and the enigmatic life and death of Jane Stanford
events.description: Curated by Associate Curator of European Art, Patrick R. Crowley, Jane! Celebrates the aesthetic and affective excesses of Jane Stanford, the Gilded Age founder and matriarch of Stanford University. …
events.blurb: Tour the exhibit on Jane Stanford's wealth, aesthetic tastes, and complicated life at Stanford's founding.

events.description: San Jose Open Mic-When is the Open Mic? Second Wednesday of the month with a few exceptions. Please check our website to confirm. What time is the Open Mic? 8:00pm to 9:30pm with sign-ups starting at…
events.blurb: Sign up and perform stand-up comedy at the monthly open mic.

events.description: Are you ready to establish yourself as a globally recognized HR expert? Want to prepare to earn your SHRM-CP and SHRM-SCP? Learn from expert SJSU faculty, network with peers, and stay on track with a…

events.description: Step into the Vampire underworld as Blade Rave turns our venue into a reimagined night inside the iconic Bloodbath rave scene from the film Blade. Arrive early, stay late, just be on the look out for…

events.address: San Jose Japantown, San Jose, CA
events.description: Guided tour with JAMsj Executive Director exploring public art, galleries, Heinlenville history, and Chinatown and Pinoytown communities.
events.blurb: Walk through Japantown's public art and galleries with a guide exploring its history and neighboring communities.

events.title: Reading with Pets
events.address: Central Park Library - Redwood Room, 2635 Homestead Road, Santa Clara, California 95051
events.description: Kids in grades K-5 practice reading aloud to quiet therapy pets at the library to improve reading skills, pronunciation, and confidence.
events.blurb: Read aloud to therapy pets at the library to build reading skills, pronunciation, and confidence.

events.title: Bike to Boba: Tea Era
events.address: The View Teen Center, 263 Escuela Ave., Mountain View, CA 94040
events.description: Staff-led bike ride through downtown Mountain View to boba destinations with bike safety overview. Complimentary boba drink included.
events.blurb: Bike through downtown Mountain View to boba shops with a safety overview and complimentary drink included.

events.title: Numu Beading Circle with John Paul Amaral
events.address: New Museum Los Gatos (NUMU), Los Gatos, CA
events.description: Join local artist John Paul Amaral for a beading circle and conversation on its significance in Native American Indigenous cultures.
events.blurb: Bead and chat with artist John Paul Amaral about beading's significance in Native American Indigenous cultures.

events.title: Mayor's Office Hours at the Saratoga Farmers' Market
events.description: The Mayor invites you to join Office Hours at the Saratoga Farmers' Market the first Saturday of every month, from 10:00 AM to Noon.
events.blurb: Meet the mayor at the farmers' market to discuss community concerns and ideas.

events.title: Lgfw Industry Night
events.description: Food and wine industry networking event with mariachi music, tequila tasting, and happy hour pricing for hospitality professionals.
events.blurb: Network with food and wine industry pros while enjoying mariachi music, tequila tasting, and happy hour pricing.

events.title: Kids swim pass
events.description: Swim pass (one adult and one child) available as a Summer Adventure halfway prize option. Valid for Saturday, July 11, 1–4 p.m.
events.blurb: Swim with one adult and one child during a designated Saturday afternoon session.

events.title: Community & Climate: The Problem with Plastic
events.description: Author talk with Susan Keefe about plastic pollution, part of the Community & Climate lecture series on global climate action.
events.blurb: Hear author Susan Keefe discuss plastic pollution and climate action.

events.title: Board Games
events.description: Free drop-in board game session featuring strategy games, family-friendly games, and card games. Come alone or bring a group.
events.blurb: Drop in to play strategy games, family-friendly games, and card games with other players.

events.title: Kids swim pass
events.address: Mary Gomez Park & Pool, Santa Clara
events.description: Swim pass (one adult and one child) available as a Summer Adventure halfway prize option. Valid for Sunday, July 12, 1–4 p.m.
events.blurb: Bring your kid and swim together at Mary Gomez Park & Pool during designated afternoon hours.

events.title: Teen Tuesday: Learn to Sew Boba Bags
events.address: Sunnyvale Public Library, Library Program Room
events.description: Teen Librarian Gigi teaches sewing machine basics while making a fabric drink bag. Various fabrics available to choose from.
events.blurb: Learn sewing machine basics from Teen Librarian Gigi while making a fabric boba bag from fabrics of your choice.

events.title: Music Fun Under the Sun: Octopretzel
events.venue: Children's Discovery Museum of San Jose
events.address: Children's Discovery Museum of San Jose, 180 Woz Way, San Jose, CA 95110
events.description: Octopretzel musicians perform whimsical, educational music for children emphasizing connection, embodiment, and playfulness.
events.blurb: Watch Octopretzel perform educational music for kids focused on connection, embodiment, and playfulness.

events.title: Zen Coloring for Adults
events.description: Relaxing coloring session for adults with provided coloring pages, pencils, and markers on a first-come, first-served basis.
events.blurb: Color provided pages with pencils and markers in a relaxing session on a first-come, first-served basis.

events.title: Teen Tuesday: DIY Squishies
events.address: Sunnyvale Public Library, Library Program Room
events.description: Create squishies from memory foam and puffy paint. Design, cut, and paint your own squishy stress reliever to take home.
events.blurb: Design, cut, and paint your own memory-foam squishy stress reliever to take home.

events.title: Well-being Walk
events.description: Join Peer Health Educators for a campus walk using the SJSU Interactive Map! All walks begin in the SWC Wellness Lounge
events.blurb: Walk the SJSU campus with peer educators starting from the Wellness Lounge.

events.title: Well-being Walk
events.description: Join Peer Health Educators for a campus walk using the SJSU Interactive Map! All walks begin in the SWC Wellness Lounge
events.blurb: Walk the SJSU campus with peer educators starting from the Wellness Lounge.

events.title: Soccer Storytime with Bay FC
events.address: Northside Branch Library, 695 Moreland Way, Santa Clara, CA 95054

events.blurb: Hear Bay FC soccer players read stories, lead soccer activities, and meet them for photos afterward.

events.title: New Landlords in Mountain View: What to Know About Tenant Protections
events.description: Educational workshop about rent stabilization, eviction protections, and landlord responsibilities in Mountain View.
events.blurb: Understand rent stabilization, eviction protections, and landlord duties in Mountain View.

events.title: Summer Movie Night #2: Zootopia 2

events.blurb: Watch Zootopia 2 outdoors with free popcorn and community tables at El Quito Park.

events.title: Fireworks display

events.description: Professional fireworks display. View from Creekside Park, Sedgwick Elementary, or Miller Avenue near Bollinger Rd.
events.blurb: Watch a professional fireworks display from Creekside Park or nearby viewing spots.

events.title: Dash Robot Coding
events.address: Sunnyvale Public Library, Library Program Room
events.description: Learn to code the Dash robot and participate in fun coding challenges for grades 2 to 5. Limited to first 30 kids.
events.blurb: Program the Dash robot and solve coding challenges with other kids.

events.title: Preschool Little Learners
events.address: Sunnyvale Public Library, Library Program Room
events.description: Drop-in songs, stories and crafts emphasizing preschool readiness for ages 3 to 5. Held Mondays from July 6 to 27.
events.blurb: Sing songs, hear stories, and make crafts with your preschooler to build readiness skills.

events.title: Sunnyvale's July 4th Celebration
events.description: Celebrate America's 250th anniversary with live music, a Ferris wheel, amusement rides and more on Murphy Avenue.

events.title: Campbell Relay for Life 2026

events.blurb: Walk and fundraise in the community's largest cancer support event benefiting research and care.

events.blurb: Watch Professor Pepper perform a bilingual magic show for the whole family.

events.description: Celebrate 70 years of Saratoga at this free community event honoring the City's history, people, and spirit.
